LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT APEXX PHYSICIANS MEDICAL CORPORATION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE REMAINS A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER DR. ENMON WAS AN EMPLOYEE TAKING INTO ACCOUNT THE VARIOUS FACTORS THAT WEIGH FOR AND AGAINST AND PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACTS AS TO NEGLIGENT HIRING. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 437C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, COURT REPORTER: GISELLE CASEY, CSR # 8068

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT ERNEST ARNETT, M.D.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS SHIFTED THE BURDEN AS TO CAUSATION AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACTS AS TO THAT ISSUE GIVEN THE LACK OF ANY EVIDENCE THAT ANY DELAY IN SEEING PLAINTIFF CONTRIBUTED TO DR. ENMON'S MISDIAGNOSIS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 437C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, COURT REPORTER: GISELLE CASEY, CSR # 8068

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Aaron Schultz, Esq., Gallowy Lucchese Everson & Picci, 925-930-9090, for defendant and moving Apexx Physicians Medical Corporation, Inc.'s
Bradley R. Bowles, Esq., and Richard T. Bowles, Esq., Bowles & Verna LLP, 926-935-3300 for Plaintiff and opposing party
Mary E. Greene, Esq., LaFollette Johnson, 916-563-3100, for defendant and non party Catholic Healthcare West

ARGUED and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANT APEXX PHYSICIANS MEDICAL CORPORATION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
Denied, there remains a triable issue whether Dr. Enmon was an employee taking into account the various factors that weigh for and against and plaintiff has raised a triable issue of material facts as to negligent hiring. Prevailing party to submit a form of order in compliance with CCP 437C(g). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Court Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR # 8068. (301/PJB)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
G. Patrick Galloway, Gallowy Lucchese Everson & Picci, 925-930-9090, for defendant and moving party Ernest Arnett, M.D.
Bradley R. Bowles, Esq., and Richard T. Bowles, Esq., Bowles & Verna LLP, 926-935-3300 for Plaintiff and opposing party
Mary E. Greene, Esq., LaFollette Johnson, 916-563-3100, for defendant and non party Catholic Healthcare West

ARGUED and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANT ERNEST ARNETT, M.D.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
Granted, defendant has shifted the burden as to causation and plaintiff has not raised a triable issue of material facts as to that issue given the lack of any evidence that any delay in seeing plaintiff contributed to Dr. Enmon's misdiagnosis. Prevailing party to submit a form of order in compliance with CCP 437C(g). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Court Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR # 8068. (301/PJB)
